symptoms: Perineal discomfort (2nd-degree tear healing); Breast engorgement; Fatigue; Night sweats; Mild baby blues (score 9 EPDS)
medications: Ibuprofen 400mg Three times daily as required; Vitamin D 1000IU Once daily; Iron supplement 65mg Once daily; Lactation support (fenugreek) 600mg Three times daily
notes: 6-week postnatal review. SVD at 39+4 weeks, 2nd degree perineal tear repaired. Breastfeeding established – mild engorgement, latch reviewed with midwife. EPDS score 9 – monitoring commenced. Bimanual examination: uterus involuted, no uterine tenderness. Cervical smear due next month. Contraception discussed.
diagnosis: Postpartum recovery; 2nd-degree perineal tear – healing; Lactation support; Postpartum blues monitoring
recommendations: Perineal hygiene and sitz baths; Pelvic floor exercises starting now; Continue breastfeeding with lactation consultant support; Re-do EPDS in 2 weeks; Contraception counselling; Cervical smear next visit; Return immediately if EPDS worsens or suicidal ideation

symptoms: Improved perineal healing; Breastfeeding well; Fatigue improving; Mood low at times; Anxiety about infant care; EPDS 7
medications: Vitamin D 1000IU Once daily; Iron supplement 65mg Once daily
notes: 10-week review. Perineum healed. Breastfeeding established successfully – infant weight gain on track. EPDS improved to 7. Patient reports mild anxiety around infant health, reassured. Weight loss since delivery 4.2 kg. Pelvic floor exercises adherent.
diagnosis: Postpartum recovery – progressing well; Postpartum anxiety – mild
recommendations: Continue EPDS monitoring; Refer to postpartum support group; Maintain pelvic floor exercises; Gentle walking 20-30 minutes daily; Partner involvement in infant care; Follow-up in 4 weeks

symptoms: Good energy levels; Breastfeeding continuing; Occasional dyspareunia; EPDS 5; Hair shedding (telogen effluvium)
medications: Vitamin D 1000IU Once daily; Postnatal multivitamin Once daily
notes: 14-week review. Good overall recovery. EPDS 5 – no clinical depression. Patient reports hair shedding; reassured postpartum telogen effluvium is normal and self-limiting. Sexual activity resumed; mild dyspareunia with dryness – topical oestrogen pessary offered. Contraception: progesterone-only pill started.
diagnosis: Postpartum recovery – well; Telogen effluvium; Lactational dyspareunia
recommendations: Lubricant for intercourse; Topical oestrogen if dyspareunia persists; Continue progesterone-only pill; Balanced diet with protein; Follow-up in 6 weeks

symptoms: Well; Breastfeeding; Hair loss normalising; No mood concerns; Return to light exercise
medications: Postnatal multivitamin Once daily; Progesterone-only pill Once daily
notes: 20-week review. Excellent recovery. Weight now 5.3 kg below delivery weight. Breastfeeding continuing full – plan to wean at 12 months. EPDS 3. No concerns raised. Cervical smear performed today – sample sent. Exercise: swimming and postnatal yoga commenced.
diagnosis: Postpartum recovery – fully resolved; Routine postnatal follow-up
recommendations: Continue POP; Cervical smear result in 2 weeks; Progress exercise gradually; Continue breastfeeding as desired; Annual smear thereafter; Follow-up in 6 weeks

symptoms: Well; Breastfeeding reducing (introducing solids to infant); Good sleep; No mood issues; Pelvic floor strong
medications: Postnatal multivitamin Once daily; Progesterone-only pill Once daily
notes: 24-week review. Impressive recovery journey complete. Patient well. Infant thriving. Cervical smear result: normal. Transitioning from breastfeeding to mixed feeding as solids introduced. No pelvic floor symptoms. Relationship wellbeing discussed – positive.
diagnosis: Postpartum – full recovery; Breastfeeding cessation planning
recommendations: Wean breastfeeding gradually to avoid engorgement; Wear supportive bra during weaning; Continue POP; Next cervical smear in 3 years; GP annual check; Discharge from specialist care
